loops.name: RSI takeoff (recursive self-improvement)

edges.mechanism: Capable AI automates the ML research that compresses algorithmic efficiency (fixed-capability compute halving).
Efficiency gains + inference-time compute open new scaling axes and relieve the data wall, so more compute keeps buying capability.
A live scaling axis converts into raw capability — the loop closes on capability driving its own R&D.

loops.mechanism: Capability automates the AI R&D that raises capability. The ONLY reference-class-free loop: no historical instance of an artifact improving its own design faster than humans can.
loops.activation_condition: Fires only once AI matches/exceeds human experts on long-horizon (32h+) frontier ML-R&D tasks — a regime not yet entered.
loops.gauge: METR RE-bench: AI-agent vs human-expert on frontier ML R&D
loops.current_gain: Open / sub-unity: AI ~4x humans at 2h but ~0.5x at 32h — automation buys SPEED not DEPTH. SWE-bench also flattening (74.9→80.9%). Loop not closed.
loops.flip_threshold: AI ≥ human expert on 32h+ ML-R&D horizons, sustained — at which point the loop self-closes and compounds endogenously (GV11 forward: crossing this pre-2032 makes a lead unbridgeable).

loops.name: Compute–capex flywheel

edges.mechanism: Rising capability concentrates durable rent at the hardware + hyperscaler-cloud layers (NVIDIA/big-three).
Realized + expected rent underwrites the capital structure and capex raising.
Capital funds leading-edge cluster + CoWoS/HBM + onshore-fab buildout.
Leading-edge dense clusters enable the frontier training run → more capability.

loops.mechanism: Capability → rent → capital → compute buildout → more capability. The engine currently driving the whole system; runs entirely on expected, not yet realized, ROI.
loops.reference_class: Railway mania (1840s), telecom/fiber capex cycle (1998-2001), electrification capex (1900s-20s)
loops.gauge: Combined hyperscaler capex; NVIDIA DC revenue; TSMC CoWoS capacity
loops.current_gain: Accelerating hard: hyperscaler capex +63%(24)/+73%(25), NVIDIA DC revenue ~38x FY20→25, CoWoS ~10x 23→26. Dominant.
loops.flip_threshold: Demand realization (GV14) fails to validate ROI — revenue-per-token stays below infra amortization — flipping the flywheel into the B1 fragility loop at the neocloud/SPV tier.

loops.name: Commoditization / diffusion flywheel

edges.mechanism: Collapsing $/capability + open weights accelerate deployment/adoption.
Wider deployment realizes demand and revenue-per-token.
Realized demand funds the next capability push.
Each capability jump is rapidly distilled/open-weighted, compressing efficiency further (diffusion lag 2yr→3mo).

loops.mechanism: The diffusion clock: capability commoditizes faster than moats hold, driving the fastest-diffusing layer. This is the counter-force closing the OUTCOME gates (GV10, GV4).
loops.reference_class: Commoditization of PCs, Linux/open-source vs proprietary Unix, generic pharma, mechanization diffusion
loops.gauge: GV10: $/capability, algorithmic-efficiency halving time, open-weight Elo gap, diffusion lag
loops.current_gain: Accelerating: ~40x/yr price decline, efficiency halving ~every 8mo, open-weight lag ~3mo & <40 Elo, open-weight token share +10pp/yr. Dominant.
loops.flip_threshold: An RSI/decisive-capability threshold (GV11) crosses BEFORE commoditization completes → winner-take-all freezes diffusion; OR value fully migrates up-stack leaving no model-layer rent to commoditize.

loops.name: Chip-chokepoint / export-control moat

edges.mechanism: Leading-edge fab (gated upstream by EUV/EDA GV2) gates frontier training.
US decisive-capability lead reduces China's credibility as a second pole/outside option.
Low China credibility strengthens US access-leverage: controls bite because there is no substitute supplier.
Tight controls deny China tooling/chips, preserving the US/allied leading-edge concentration.

loops.mechanism: US controls the hardest gates (GV2 EUV, GV1 fabs) → denies rivals → preserves the lead → sustains the coalition that keeps controls tight. Even number of negatives → net reinforcing.
loops.reference_class: COCOM Cold-War tech-denial regime; CoCom/Wassenaar dual-use controls
loops.gauge: GV4: Entity-list growth, NVIDIA China share, Singapore transshipment, smuggled-GPU stock, Ascend shipments
loops.current_gain: Holding at the leading edge (Entity list ~5.5x, NVIDIA China 26→13%, ASML ~100% EUV) but bite eroding — Singapore billing 8.5→22%, smuggling accelerating (~660k H100e), Ascend doubling.
loops.flip_threshold: China indigenizes EUV at volume (GV2 prototype→production, ~2026-28 window) OR efficiency/open-weight substitution (GV10) makes leading-edge chips non-decisive — either voids the moat.

loops.name: Talent concentration flywheel

edges.mechanism: Power-law-concentrated frontier talent produces the capability frontier.
Capability generates the rent/comp pools that fund talent.
Rent funds $100M+ comp + ecosystem gravity that attracts and retains the researcher core.

loops.mechanism: Talent → capability → rent → comp → talent. Ecosystem agglomeration self-reinforces, but leaks at the immigration margin.
loops.reference_class: Fairchild→Silicon Valley agglomeration; 1930s émigré-physicist concentration; Bell Labs
loops.gauge: GV12: US elite-researcher work-location share, stay-rate, comp packages, green-card backlog
loops.current_gain: Container holds: US work-location share stuck ~51-59%, comp up ~2 orders of magnitude — but self-sabotaging: stay-rate 90→76%, green-card backlog widening, China now #1 undergrad-origin source.
loops.flip_threshold: Stay-rate + H-1B/green-card friction crosses the point where net elite-talent flow reverses (China undergrad-origin share already leads) — container empties.

loops.name: Demand/ROI capital-fragility ceiling

edges.mechanism: Capital funds capacity buildout.
Capacity outrunning any killer-app revenue widens the ROI gap (supply without realized demand).
Demand shortfall triggers impairment at the fragile neocloud/SPV tier → capital withdraws → buildout cut.

loops.mechanism: The balancing counterpart to R2: capacity built ahead of demand forces a capital retrenchment. Latent now; hyperscalers self-fund and are insulated, fragility concentrates at GPU-collateral SPVs.
loops.activation_condition: Bites once revenue-per-token stays below infra amortization at scale — the ROI miss.
loops.reference_class: Telecom/fiber glut 2000-02; railway mania bust; 1980s S&L
loops.gauge: GV14 (no killer app, revenue-per-token) + GV16 (neocloud/SPV fragility, circular vendor financing)
loops.current_gain: Latent, not yet triggered — hyperscalers insulated by $50B+/yr FCF; fragility ring-fenced at the SPV tier (impairment within quarters if it fires).
loops.flip_threshold: Revenue-per-token < infra amortization sustained ~2-3 quarters at scale → neocloud impairment cascade transmits to the core capex plan.

loops.name: Power/grid execution ceiling

edges.mechanism: Buildout ambition consumes grid execution headroom — DC load doubling every 3-4yr floods the interconnection queue.
Available power gates deployment scale (queue 55-60mo, HV transformer ~130wk).
Capped deployment throttles the revenue/scale funding the next capability round.

loops.mechanism: Rising buildout depletes grid headroom faster than it can be added, capping realized scale. The decisive US minimum stave — one negative link → balancing.
loops.reference_class: US electrification grid buildout; 1960s-70s generation-capacity crunch; California 2000-01
loops.gauge: GV6: interconnection queue, request-to-COD time, LPT lead time, DC demand, HV transmission miles
loops.current_gain: Binding and tightening: queue widening, transformer lead +10-15wk/yr, transmission stuck ~80% below 2010s pace, DC demand accelerating. THE US binding constraint.
loops.flip_threshold: Interconnection reform + behind-the-meter gas/nuclear + SMRs clear the queue faster than demand grows (only partial by ~2030) — until then the ceiling holds.

loops.name: Minerals counter-lever MAD

edges.mechanism: US chip-control escalation triggers China rare-earth/gallium/magnet weaponization (mutual chokepoints).
Minerals + grid-equipment (transformer) restriction raises US buildout cost (+15-25%) and timeline (+6-12mo).
US buildout pain forces softening of chip controls at the margin — the deterrent restrains escalation.

loops.mechanism: Symmetric chokepoints (US chips ↔ China minerals) create a MAD-like stalemate — one net-negative link → balancing/deterrent, not a spiral.
loops.activation_condition: Bites when either side escalates its chokepoint; dormant under the current managed truce.
loops.reference_class: OPEC 1973 embargo; resource-weaponization episodes; MAD arms-control stalemate
loops.gauge: GV8: rare-earth processing share, gallium price, magnet-export switch, US transformer lead
loops.current_gain: Active but bounded lever: processing stuck ~87-91%, gallium +7-9x, magnet exports switched -75%→+157% on policy — raises cost/timeline, doesn't stop buildout.
loops.flip_threshold: Ex-China processing reaches ~30-40% (removes the lever, ~post-2028) OR US chip control becomes so decisive China accepts full escalation — either breaks the stalemate.

loops.name: Fiscal-dominance / bond-market loop

edges.mechanism: Higher r*/savings scarcity (AI+energy capex + rearmament) raises the yield needed to clear the marginal UST buyer.
Loss of the marginal UST bid forces higher yields → fiscal stress → pressures CB independence toward fiscal dominance.
Fiscal dominance selects the inflate-away/repression adjustment path.
Repression/inflate-away suppresses realized real rates below r* by force, closing the loop.

loops.mechanism: AI+energy capex raises r*, the shrinking sovereign UST bid forces the choice, and repression suppresses rates — reinforcing DRIFT toward soft financial repression unless productivity intervenes.
loops.reference_class: Post-WWII financial repression 1945-51; 1970s inflate-away; emerging-market fiscal dominance
loops.gauge: GV39 r* drift, GV37 dollar/UST bid, GV38 plumbing stress, GV36 CB-independence
loops.current_gain: Drifting toward soft repression: r* upward-sticky, dollar share ~58% slow-eroding with a strong floor, GV38 fragile (basis trade $600B-1T, SLR-capped dealers). Not yet realized.
loops.flip_threshold: AI productivity offset (GV40) arrives contemporaneously → deficits self-liquidate and break the loop; OR Treasury-plumbing (GV38) non-linear stress forces a Fed backstop within hours, ratcheting the regime instead.

loops.name: Labor reinstatement / underconsumption brake

edges.mechanism: A higher capability ceiling substitutes more cognitive labor, depressing the reinstatement (task-creation) ratio.
A falling reinstatement ratio compresses wage income/appropriability (labor-share compression + monopsony).
Compressed wages + MPC differential mechanically cut aggregate demand → underconsumption caps demand realization.
The demand shortfall undercuts the revenue base funding the capability buildout.

loops.mechanism: Automation displacing labor erodes its own demand base (underconsumption) — the classical balancing brake. Grounded in mechanization, NOT theoretical; buffered by the diffusion lag (GV13).
loops.activation_condition: Bites once displacement outruns reinstatement AND the consumer-credit buffer that masked prior wage stagnation is exhausted.
loops.reference_class: Mechanization/automation waves (textiles, farm, factory) — reinstatement with 15-30yr lags; Luddite immiseration decades
loops.gauge: GV17 reinstatement ratio, GV18 wage-share/appropriability, GV13 diffusion lag
loops.current_gain: Latent and indeterminate: entry-level knowledge-work contraction + apprenticeship-severance signals, but no clear net destruction yet — buffered by the deployment lag (GV13).
loops.flip_threshold: Reinstatement ratio <1 sustained + credit buffer exhausts + MPC differential bites → aggregate-demand contraction feeds back onto the buildout.

couplings.contest: The master clock. R1 (RSI) pushes GV11 toward a self-closing takeoff that freezes the field; R3 (diffusion) pushes the SAME capability out into commoditized open weights that erase any lead. Opposite directions on the single node the whole worldview hangs on.
couplings.dominance_rule: Diffusion (R3) dominant NOW: RE-bench stuck (AI 0.5x humans at 32h), SWE-bench flattening 75→81% — RSI loop is open, while the diffusion gauges compound (efficiency halving 8mo, lag 2yr→3mo). Flips only if AI crosses ≥human on 32h+ ML-R&D horizons, after which R1 compounds endogenously and R3 is frozen out.
couplings.evidence_for: Every RSI gauge reads sub-unity at long horizons and is stuck; every diffusion gauge is accelerating. The tail (deep, long-horizon reasoning) is exactly where automation fails.
couplings.steelman_against: METR 50%-task horizon is doubling ~every 4 months post-2023 — non-linear. A single architecture jump could carry the 32h horizon suddenly, and a lead held THROUGH the threshold is unbridgeable, so the low-probability branch has unbounded payoff.

couplings.contest: R2 needs leading-edge fabs to keep the capex flywheel producing frontier TRAINING; R4 controls them as the moat; R3 argues efficiency/open-weights route around them. Contest over whether the gated input binds the whole value chain.
couplings.dominance_rule: Leading-edge chips bind TRAINING, not INFERENCE. The moat holds at the training frontier (SMIC stuck at DUV yield wall, TSMC 2-node gap stable, ASML ~100% EUV) but is irrelevant to the inference/efficiency layer where GV10 operates. Flips if China indigenizes EUV at volume (2026-28) — then the constraint releases at the training frontier too.
couplings.evidence_for: GV2 forward: leading-edge gate holds >90% probability to 2028; SMIC yield collapses each node step (90%→50% 14→7nm). Meanwhile DeepSeek-class training at ~$5-6M shows the inference/efficiency path is unconstrained by sub-5nm.
couplings.steelman_against: If frontier capability keeps requiring ever-larger dense training clusters, the training constraint IS the whole-chain constraint — inference cheapness is downstream of a frontier only leading-edge fabs can produce.

couplings.contest: R4 tightens export-control bite as the moat; R3 says open weights + algorithmic efficiency substitute around the control premise, making the chokepoint control a diminishing asset even as it tightens on paper.
couplings.dominance_rule: Control tightening on HARDWARE vectors (Entity list 5.5x, FDPR, NVIDIA China 26→13%) but bite ERODING on outcomes: Singapore transshipment 8.5→22%, smuggled stock ~660k H100e accelerating, algo efficiency halving 8mo. Substitution wins over the window because the controlled variable (chips) is decoupling from the decisive variable (capability).
couplings.evidence_for: GV4 gauge shows the hardware ratchet and the leak accelerating simultaneously; GV10 explicitly substitutes_for GV4 in the edge set.
couplings.steelman_against: Controls only need to hold at the very frontier (the largest training runs) to deny a decisive lead; commoditized capability is by definition non-frontier, so leakage of trailing capability doesn't break the moat that matters.

couplings.contest: R2's flywheel captures rent to fund itself; R3's commoditization erases model-layer rent. They meet at the value-capture locus and push it in opposite directions.
couplings.dominance_rule: Rent migrates UP-STACK, not away: as the model layer commoditizes (API price -95-98%), durable rent concentrates at hardware + hyperscaler-cloud (NVIDIA margin stuck-high 73-75%, big-three cloud flat ~63% of a market growing 35%). The flywheel survives commoditization by relocating where value accrues.
couplings.evidence_for: GV15 gauges: NVIDIA DC revenue 38x with margins holding, cloud concentration flat while the base commoditizes — value in USE and in non-replicable layers, not in intelligence-creation.
couplings.steelman_against: If open-weight + efficiency collapse inference cost toward marginal-electricity, even hardware/cloud rent compresses — the value locus shifts past the hyperscalers to end-user applications no incumbent controls, breaking R2's funding.

couplings.contest: Same node, same variable, opposite sign depending on ROI: R2 treats capital as self-reinforcing (rent funds capex), B1 treats it as a fragility ceiling (capacity ahead of demand forces retrenchment).
couplings.dominance_rule: R2 dominant NOW: capex accelerating, hyperscalers self-fund from $50B+/yr FCF and are insulated. B1 is ring-fenced at the neocloud/SPV tier (GPU-collateral, circular financing). Flips to B1 dominance on a demand miss — revenue-per-token below amortization for 2-3 quarters transmits impairment from SPVs to the core plan.
couplings.evidence_for: GV16: bifurcated structure — core insulated, fragility concentrated and fast-transmitting at the SPV tier. GV14: no killer app yet underwrites 2027-30 infra.
couplings.steelman_against: Circular vendor financing (chipmaker→neocloud→hyperscaler) means the tiers are not actually ring-fenced; an SPV impairment cascade could mark down the collateral the whole stack is priced against, pulling the core in.

couplings.contest: The productivity offset is the shared hinge: B2 (power ceiling) delays the diffusion that produces the offset; B4 (fiscal dominance) is broken only if the offset arrives to self-liquidate deficits. Power constraint upstream starves the fiscal escape downstream.
couplings.dominance_rule: Offset NOT visible now (GV40 in lag/uncertain band; capex contemporaneous, TFP not yet). Because B2 keeps grid the binding constraint (queue widening), diffusion — and thus the offset — is delayed, so B4's repression drift continues unopposed. The two balancing loops compound into a worse macro outcome.
couplings.evidence_for: GV6 gates GV40 (no offset without power); GV40 gates GV36. Grid gauges all widening; TFP invisible; r* upward-sticky, dollar bid shrinking.
couplings.steelman_against: Energy-cost collapse (GV7 solar+storage sub-$50/MWh, Wright's Law) plus behind-the-meter siting could bypass the transmission constraint and deliver the offset fast, self-liquidating deficits and breaking B4 before repression is realized.

couplings.contest: R5's talent flywheel reinforces the US moat (retained researchers = capability lead = R4). But talent is also a substitute for chip control — extraction/emigration transfers tacit process knowledge that partially substitutes for GV4. Same node cuts both ways.
couplings.dominance_rule: US container holds NOW (work-location share stuck 51-59%, comp up 2 orders of magnitude), reinforcing R4. But the asymmetric leak — stay-rate 90→76%, green-card backlog widening, China #1 undergrad-origin source — is the substitution channel that erodes the moat from the talent side, not the chip side. Flips if net elite-talent flow reverses.
couplings.evidence_for: GV12 gauges: destination share flat (container holds) but every mobility gauge eroding; edge set: GV12 substitutes_for GV4 (tacit-knowledge transfer) and GV12 gates GV34 (seizing a lab captures a shell if researchers leave).
couplings.steelman_against: Ecosystem gravity + $100M comp are structurally inelastic advantages a 5-7yr PhD pipeline can't erode quickly; the destination-share gauge (the one that matters) hasn't budged in 8 years, so the leak is noise.

couplings.contest: R4's moat depends on China being a non-credible outside option (US leverage-through-access holds only while GV27 low). R3's diffusion — China's deliberate open-weight release + Digital Silk Road + Ascend fallback — is asymmetric adoption that rebuilds China's second-pole credibility, reopening the auction and voiding the leverage.
couplings.dominance_rule: Genuinely contested. US access-leverage holds while China stays non-credible (GV11 lead), but active Chinese suitorship — deliberate open-weight diffusion, Ascend A100-tier fallback installed in 100+ countries — sustains a real two-sided auction (GV27). The gauge is the most balanced of the set; dominance flips on whether the Global-South standards contest tips China's way.
couplings.evidence_for: GV27: genuine auction ongoing; GV11 gates GV27 (winner-take-all would collapse it) but GV11 has no decisive threshold by 2028; China open-weight token share rising, DeepSeek credible.
couplings.steelman_against: Ascend is HBM-ceiling-bound and A100-tier — a fallback, not parity; as long as the US holds the frontier, China's 'credibility' is a marketing artifact and buyers still queue for NVIDIA, so US leverage is more durable than the auction framing implies.

theory_templates.name: Dalio Big Cycle (empire / debt / reserve-currency)
theory_templates.governs: The outermost boundary condition: the monetary-fiscal substrate (B4) and the second-pole / reserve-currency contest (GV27, GV36-42). Sets the macro envelope every faster loop runs inside — the cost of capital, the credibility of the marginal UST bid, and the geopolitical stakes of the frontier race.
phases: Top / peak (debt bubble, wealth gaps, financialization, challenger rises)
Decline (fiscal dominance, monetization/repression, internal + external conflict)
theory_templates.where_we_are: Late-top / early-decline for the incumbent reserve power: debt-service and AI+rearmament capex raise r*, the sovereign UST bid shrinks, and the system drifts toward soft financial repression (B4). A credible challenger (China) is rising but not yet a credible second pole (GV27 contested).
theory_templates.base_rate_shape: Multi-decade arc that resolves fiscal excess through inflate-away / financial repression rather than default; reserve status erodes slowly with a strong floor unless a plumbing break or war forces a discrete regime change.

theory_templates.name: Perez techno-economic surge / financial-capital cycle
theory_templates.governs: The tempo of the compute-capex buildout and its diffusion: R2 (capex flywheel), B1 (capital fragility), R3 (commoditization/diffusion), GV15 value-locus. Nested inside the Dalio envelope — the surge is being financed under late-cycle monetary conditions.
phases: Frenzy / installation (financial-capital mania, over-build, decoupling from fundamentals)
Turning point (crash / shakeout — the ROI reckoning)
Synergy / deployment (production-capital golden age, broad diffusion)
theory_templates.where_we_are: Late frenzy / installation: hyperscaler capex +63/73% YoY, NVIDIA DC revenue ~38x, CoWoS ~10x — a classic financial-capital mania running on expected not realized ROI, approaching the turning-point test (GV14 demand validation).
theory_templates.base_rate_shape: Installation frenzy over-builds physical capacity, hits a turning-point crash (railways 1840s, telecom/fiber 2000-02), then the cheap over-built capacity powers a longer deployment golden age. Bust is modal-but-not-certain; deployment is near-certain eventually.

theory_templates.name: Bostrom RSI loop-gain / decisive strategic advantage
theory_templates.governs: The innermost, fastest loop: R1 (recursive self-improvement) and its coupling to GV11. Fires inside — and only if permitted by — the Perez buildout and Dalio envelope. Quarantined: its dynamics do not touch the base case unless the crossover threshold is crossed.
phases: Recalcitrance / slow (loop gain sub-unity, human-paced R&D)
Crossover (AI matches human expert at long-horizon R&D)
theory_templates.where_we_are: Pre-crossover, loop open and sub-unity: AI ~4x humans at 2h but ~0.5x at 32h ML-R&D; SWE-bench flattening 75->81%. Automation buys speed, not depth. The loop is not closed.
theory_templates.base_rate_shape: Reference-class-free and bimodal: either the loop stays open and fizzles into the diffusion regime, or gain crosses unity and compounds hard into a winner-take-all takeoff. No smooth middle.

theory_templates.name: Acemoglu-Restrepo task displacement vs reinstatement
theory_templates.governs: The labor / demand base: B5 (reinstatement / underconsumption brake), GV17 reinstatement ratio, GV18 wage-share. Runs on a diffusion-lagged clock (GV13) inside the Perez deployment phase.
phases: Immiseration interlude (labor share falls, lagged before new tasks appear)
theory_templates.where_we_are: Early displacement: entry-level knowledge-work contraction and apprenticeship-severance signals, but no clear net destruction yet — buffered by the deployment lag (GV13). Reinstatement ratio indeterminate.
theory_templates.base_rate_shape: Historically reinstatement arrives but with 15-30yr lags and multi-decade immiseration interludes (textiles, farm, factory). The brake bites only if displacement outruns reinstatement AND the consumer-credit buffer exhausts.

driving_forces.name: The master capability clock (RSI vs diffusion)
driving_forces.reading: Diffusion (R3) dominant and accelerating (efficiency halving ~8mo, open-weight lag 2yr->3mo, price -40x/yr); RSI (R1) open and sub-unity at long horizons. The single node the whole worldview hangs on (GV11) is being pushed toward commoditization, not takeoff.

driving_forces.name: Compute-capital buildout engine
driving_forces.reading: R2 flywheel accelerating hard and self-funding at the core (hyperscaler $50B+/yr FCF, insulated); B1 fragility latent and ring-fenced at the neocloud/SPV tier. Runs entirely on expected ROI awaiting demand validation (GV14).

driving_forces.name: Physical chokepoint lattice (chips <-> minerals <-> electrons)
driving_forces.reading: Mutual-assured-disruption standoff: US chip moat (R4) holds at the training frontier but bite erodes on outcomes; China minerals lever (B3) bounded, raises US cost not stops it; power/grid (B2) is the binding US minimum stave, tightening. Each pole holds a chokepoint on the other.

driving_forces.name: Value-capture locus (up-stack migration)
driving_forces.reading: Rent is not disappearing but relocating up-stack: model layer commoditizing (API -95-98%) while durable rent concentrates at hardware + hyperscaler-cloud + power (NVIDIA margin 73-75%, cloud concentration flat). The physical frontier winner is often not the value winner.

driving_forces.name: Talent concentration and second-pole credibility
driving_forces.reading: US container holds on the gauge that matters (work-location share stuck 51-59%, comp +2 orders of magnitude) but every mobility gauge leaks (stay-rate 90->76%, green-card backlog widening, China #1 undergrad-origin source). China rebuilding second-pole credibility (GV27) via deliberate open-weight diffusion + Ascend fallback.

driving_forces.name: Monetary-fiscal substrate
driving_forces.reading: Drifting toward soft financial repression: r* upward-sticky under AI+rearmament capex, dollar share ~58% slow-eroding with a strong floor, Treasury plumbing fragile (basis trade $600B-1T, SLR-capped dealers). Bifurcates by capital-account regime — US faces open-market discipline, China resolves the same pressure quietly.

driving_forces.name: Labor / aggregate-demand base
driving_forces.reading: Latent and indeterminate underconsumption brake: early displacement signals but reinstatement ratio not yet clearly <1, and the consumer-credit buffer not yet exhausted. Buffered by the diffusion lag; the slow-fuse check on whether the buildout starves its own demand.

branches.name: Base case — diffusion-deployment, no decisive threshold
branches.trigger_threshold: GV11 crosses no decisive-capability threshold before ~2032; diffusion clock (R3) outruns every moat; controls hold at the frontier but leak on outcomes; power ceiling (B2) throttles but does not break the buildout.
branches.shape: Perez frenzy matures through a turbulent-but-survivable turning-point into a messy multipolar deployment. Value migrates relentlessly up-stack; chip<->mineral MAD standoff holds; monetary system drifts into soft financial repression as the productivity offset arrives late (B2 starves GV40).
branches.who_wins: Distributed and contested: owners of power, compute, cloud aggregation, and the reorganized application layer capture durable rent — NOT necessarily the frontier-fabrication or frontier-model holder. No uncontested path for any single actor; open-weight capability floor persists.

branches.name: RSI takeoff — decisive strategic advantage (THEORETICAL, quarantined)
branches.trigger_threshold: AI matches/exceeds human experts on sustained 32h+ frontier ML-R&D horizons (METR RE-bench crosses unity) BEFORE ~2032 — a regime not yet entered.
branches.shape: Loop gain crosses unity; R1 self-closes and compounds endogenously. Diffusion (R3) is frozen out as capability outruns commoditization; R2 and R4 lock into winner-take-all. Recalcitrance -> crossover -> fast takeoff -> singleton.
branches.who_wins: The lab / nation holding the lead THROUGH the threshold — unbridgeable and winner-take-all. Its loops never touch the base case; treat as a separate quarantined world, not a probability-averaged input.

branches.name: Buildout bust — Perez turning-point / ROI reckoning
branches.trigger_threshold: Revenue-per-token stays below infra amortization at scale for ~2-3 sustained quarters (GV14 miss) -> neocloud/SPV impairment cascade transmits through circular vendor financing to the core capex plan.
branches.shape: Frenzy flips into turning-point crash a la telecom/fiber 2000-02: SPV-tier impairment marks down GPU collateral, capex retrenches, frontier training starves. Bust is followed — Perez-style — by a deployment golden age on cheap over-built capacity. Can co-detonate B4 via a growth/credit shock.
branches.who_wins: Survivors with balance-sheet insulation (hyperscalers on $50B+ FCF) and distressed-asset buyers; frontier labs dependent on external capital and the neocloud/SPV tier are wiped. Value locus shifts down toward end-users on cheapened compute.

branches.name: Exogenous fracture — Taiwan / kinetic shock
branches.trigger_threshold: Taiwan/TSMC (GV21) discontinuity — kinetic conflict, blockade, or fab-offline event — and/or full mutual chip<->mineral escalation past the MAD deterrent.
branches.shape: The one discontinuity that zeroes both poles at once: leading-edge supply and mineral/grid supply both cut, monetary layer detonated directly (B4 plumbing break -> forced Fed backstop). Rapid forced decoupling, capex freeze, regime-change in the monetary order.
branches.who_wins: Nobody cleanly; relative winners are whoever holds reshored/redundant fabrication, mineral processing, and grid capacity. Catastrophic for the frontier race on both sides; accelerates the Dalio external-conflict phase.

tail_ignitions.tail: RSI hard takeoff freezes the field
tail_ignitions.threshold: AI >= human expert on sustained 32h+ ML-R&D horizons (RE-bench crosses unity) pre-2032
tail_ignitions.what_it_resets: Converts the whole system from a diffusion regime to winner-take-all; the lead held through the threshold becomes unbridgeable and R3 is frozen out. Resets the entire value and geopolitical map.

tail_ignitions.tail: Capex flywheel flips to fragility cascade
tail_ignitions.threshold: Revenue-per-token < infra amortization at scale, sustained ~2-3 quarters
tail_ignitions.what_it_resets: SPV impairment cascade transmits through circular vendor financing to the core capex plan; resets the capital structure and forces the Perez turning-point, moving the value locus down-stack.

tail_ignitions.tail: Taiwan / TSMC discontinuity zeroes both poles
tail_ignitions.threshold: GV21 kinetic/blockade/fab-offline event, or full chip<->mineral escalation past the B3 deterrent
tail_ignitions.what_it_resets: Simultaneously cuts leading-edge supply and mineral/grid supply and detonates the monetary layer; resets the global supply chain and accelerates decoupling and the Dalio external-conflict phase.

tail_ignitions.threshold: Treasury-plumbing (GV38) non-linear stress forcing a Fed backstop within hours, before the productivity offset (GV40) arrives
tail_ignitions.what_it_resets: Ratchets the monetary regime toward realized financial repression / fiscal dominance and undermines CB independence; resets the cost of capital envelope every faster loop runs inside.

tail_ignitions.tail: Elite-talent flow reversal empties the US container
tail_ignitions.threshold: Stay-rate + H-1B/green-card friction crosses the point where net elite-talent flow reverses (China undergrad-origin share already leads)
tail_ignitions.what_it_resets: Erodes the US moat from the talent side (tacit-knowledge transfer substitutes for chip control), rebuilding China's second-pole credibility (GV27) and voiding US access-leverage independent of the hardware gate.

dominant_dynamic: The master-clock contest at GV11: the RSI reinforcing loop (R1) pushing capability toward a self-closing, field-freezing takeoff versus the diffusion/commoditization flywheel (R3) pushing that same capability out into open weights that erase every lead. Diffusion is winning now — every RSI gauge reads sub-unity and stuck at long horizons while every diffusion gauge compounds — so the decisive question is purely one of timing: does a decisive-capability threshold arrive before commoditization completes (pre-2032), or does the diffusion clock run the table.
system_dominant_loop: R2 — the compute-capex flywheel. It is the engine literally moving trillions right now (capex +63/73% YoY, NVIDIA DC revenue ~38x, CoWoS ~10x) and every other loop is currently reacting to the buildout it drives; R3 (diffusion) is its pervasive erosive counter-force, but R2 is what sets the system's tempo and stakes today.
current_phase: Perez late frenzy / installation approaching the turning-point test, nested inside a Dalio late-top / early-decline reserve-currency phase (fiscal-dominance drift), with the Bostrom clock still pre-crossover (RSI loop open). A financial-capital mania on expected ROI, running into a power/grid ceiling and a demand-validation reckoning.
macro_trajectory: A frenzy-phase capex mania colliding with two hard ceilings — power/grid execution (B2) and demand/ROI validation (B1) — while the diffusion clock relentlessly commoditizes the model layer and pushes durable rent up-stack toward power, compute, cloud, and the reorganized application layer. The modal shape is a turbulent turning-point wobble (partial shakeout, not clean takeoff nor clean deployment) resolving into a messy, multipolar deployment under soft financial repression, with the physical-frontier winner frequently NOT the value winner. Overhanging this base arc are two discrete detonators — a Taiwan fracture that zeroes both poles at once, and (quarantined, never averaged in) an RSI takeoff that would freeze the field into winner-take-all.
watch_thresholds: METR RE-bench / SWE-bench: AI vs human-expert on 32h+ ML-R&D horizons — crossing unity ignites branch B (R1)
Revenue-per-token vs infra amortization at scale, sustained 2-3 quarters — the GV14 demand-validation gate that flips R2 into B1 (branch C)
Interconnection queue length, request-to-COD time, HV transformer lead time — the binding US power ceiling (B2)
China EUV indigenization prototype->volume production (2026-28 window) — voids the training-frontier moat (R4)
Ex-China mineral/rare-earth processing share reaching ~30-40% — removes China's counter-lever (B3, ~post-2028)
US elite-researcher stay-rate and net-flow direction; China undergrad-origin share — the talent-container leak (R5)
r* drift, dollar/UST marginal bid, Treasury basis-trade and dealer-balance-sheet stress (GV38) — fiscal-dominance ratchet (B4)
Open-weight token share and diffusion lag (currently ~3mo) — the pace of the R3 commoditization clock
Taiwan/TSMC status (GV21) — the single discontinuity gating branch D
Reinstatement ratio (<1 sustained) + consumer-credit-buffer exhaustion — the labor/underconsumption brake (B5)
synthesis: The systems model says this is not a single race with one winner but a chokepoint lattice running against two clocks under a late-cycle monetary envelope. The slow Dalio boundary condition (fiscal-dominance drift, contested second-pole) sets the cost of capital and the geopolitical stakes; the medium Perez cycle (R2 capex flywheel now dominant, B1 fragility latent) sets the tempo and the coming turning-point test; the fast Bostrom loop (R1) is quarantined and open. These nest — the outer bounds the inner — and must NOT be averaged. The dominant contest is R1 vs R3 at GV11, and R3 (diffusion) is winning decisively today, which is why the modal world (A, ~0.55) is messy multipolar deployment in which durable rent migrates up-stack to power/compute/cloud/apps and the physical-frontier holder is often not the value winner. Because leading-edge chips bind training but not inference, because export controls tighten on hardware while leaking on outcomes, and because power is the true US binding stave taxed by China's mineral lever, no actor holds an uncontested path: security-denial at the frontier can succeed while the paradigm still 'fails' on diffusion, commercial value-capture, and labor reinstatement simultaneously. The forecast is therefore a shape plus quarantined tails, not a point: base-case up-stack deployment under soft repression (0.55), a Perez ROI bust (0.20), a Taiwan fracture that zeroes both poles (0.15), and — held strictly apart — the low-probability, unbounded-payoff RSI takeoff (0.10). Watch the RE-bench long-horizon crossing and the revenue-per-token/amortization gate above all else: the first switches on the quarantined world, the second tips the base case into the bust.
